pattern: Walk all 8 directions from every cell of a small grid, collect multi-digit numbers formed along each ray, count primes greater than 10, and return the most frequent (largest on tie).
hints: From each cell, extend rays in all 8 compass directions; at each step, the current running number is prev_number * 10 + digit.
Use a sieve or trial division to test primality — since the grid is at most 6×6 with digits 1–9, numbers along a ray have at most 6 digits (≤ 999999), so trial division up to sqrt(n) is fast.
Tally every prime > 10 in a frequency map; then scan for the maximum frequency and return the largest prime with that frequency, or -1 if the map is empty.

edge_inputs.description: 1×1 grid — only single-digit numbers, no prime > 10
all 1s — 11 is the only qualifying prime
6×6 grid all 9s — largest prime wins among tied frequencies
grid with no prime > 10 reachable (all composite multi-digit combos)
tie in frequency — return the larger prime

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = size
    n = size
    mat = [[rng.randint(1, 9) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [mat]
